title: Job Vacancies at Uncle Stan's Food
content:
Uncle Stans Food is a quick service restaurant (QSR),a fast-food model built for speed and convenience, featuring a limited, standardized menu of items like small shops or pizza. Operating primarily through counter-service and drive-thrus, it focuses on rapid food assembly from pre-prepared components, ensuring consistent, affordable meals delivered in minutes. Our functional, casual environment prioritizes efficient turnover over prolonged dining, making it a dominant global industry by fulfilling the demand for reliable, on-the-go satisfaction.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Restaurant Supervisor

Job Location: Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Summary


	We are seeking an experienced and dedicated Restaurant Supervisor to oversee daily operations at our Quick Service Restaurant (QSR) outlet.
	The ideal candidate must have a strong background in QSR supervision, excellent leadership skills, and the ability to ensure smooth and efficient restaurant operations.
	Applicants must reside on the Island, possess strong organizational abilities, and be capable of maintaining high service and operational standards.


Key Responsibilities


	Supervise and coordinate daily restaurant operations to ensure efficiency.
	Oversee and support staff performance, ensuring adherence to company standards.
	Maintain excellent customer service and promptly handle customer inquiries and complaints.
	Ensure accurate order processing and timely service delivery.
	Monitor inventory levels, restocking, and usage of supplies.
	Ensure compliance with food safety, hygiene, and operational guidelines.
	Participate in staff training, coaching, and performance evaluation.
	Communicate effectively with kitchen and service teams to ensure smooth workflow.
	Ensure the restaurant environment is clean, organized, and well-maintained at all times.
	Prepare and submit operational reports as required.
	Support management in driving sales, maintaining outlet standards, and enhancing customer satisfaction.


Requirements


	Minimum of an HND or B.Sc Degree in any relevant field.
	2 - 3 years of proven experience as a Restaurant Supervisor in a Quick Service Restaurant (QSR) is mandatory.
	Strong leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to multitask and perform in a fast-paced environment.
	Strong attention to detail and problem-solving skills.
	Must be punctual, reliable, and result-oriented.
	Must reside on the Island or within close proximity to the outlet.
	Professional appearance and positive attitude


Salary
N180,000 monthly. 

Application Closing Date: 2nd May, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position  as the subject of the email.

 

2.) Customer Service Representative

Job Locations: Ikoyi, Admiralty Lekki Phase 1, Osapa, and Surulere, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Customer Service Representative will be responsible for providing excellent customer service, taking and processing orders accurately, and ensuring a positive dining experience for customers.
	The ideal candidate should be professional, attentive, and able to work efficiently in a fast-paced Quick Service Restaurant (QSR) environment.


Key Responsibilities


	Welcome and attend to customers in a friendly and professional manner.
	Take customer orders accurately and ensure proper order processing.
	Process customer orders accurately (online and in-person)
	Handle customer inquiries, feedback, and complaints professionally.
	Ensure a smooth and excellent experience for every customer
	Ensure orders are delivered accurately and promptly.
	Manage phone calls, emails, and social media inquiries
	Maintain updated knowledge of menu items and promotions
	Ensure effective communication between the service area and kitchen staff.
	Support daily outlet operations and collaborate effectively with team members.
	Uphold the company’s customer service standards and brand reputation.


Requirements


	Minimum of a B.Sc. degree in any relevant field.
	Proven experience in customer service within a Quick Service Restaurant (QSR) environment is preferred.
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to work efficiently in a fast-paced environment.
	Professional appearance and positive attitude.
	Strong attention to detail and good problem-solving skills.
	Must be punctual, reliable, and able to work effectively within a team.
	Candidates must reside close to the outlet location for which they are applying for.


Salary
N130,000 per month.

Application Closing Date: 28th April, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

 

 

3.) Storekeeper

Job Location: Ikoyi, Osapa / Orchid, or Admiralty Road (Lekki) - Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Store Keeper will be responsible for managing inventory, tracking stock levels, receiving and issuing goods, and ensuring that all store operations are carried out accurately and efficiently.
	The ideal candidate must be organized, detail-oriented, and able to maintain proper documentation of all store activities.


Key Responsibilities


	Receive, inspect, and record incoming stock and materials
	Issue stock to various departments based on approved requisitions
	Maintain accurate inventory records and update stock levels daily
	Conduct regular stock counts and report discrepancies immediately
	Ensure proper storage, labeling, and arrangement of items for easy retrieval
	Monitor stock usage to prevent shortages or overstocking
	Maintain cleanliness and organization of the store area
	Prepare and submit inventory reports to management
	Ensure compliance with company policies and safety standards
	Work closely with the procurement and operations teams to ensure seamless supply flow


Qualifications


	OND / HND / B.Sc in any relevant field
	1–3 years of experience as a Store Keeper or in store / inventory management
	Strong knowledge of inventory control procedures
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and inventory software is an added advantage
	Excellent organization and record-keeping skills
	Strong attention to detail and accuracy
	Ability to work in a fast-paced environment
	Good communication and interpersonal skills
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team


Important Requirements:


	Applicants must reside within Ikoyi, Osapa/Orchid, or Admiralty Road (Lekki).
	Only candidates living in these areas will be considered.
	This requirement is mandatory due to proximity to our outlet locations.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary of ?130,000 monthly
	Opportunities for growth and career development
	Supportive and friendly work environment.


Application Closing Date: 30th April, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

     

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Openings at Baker Hughes
content:
Baker Hughes, a GE company (NYSE:BHGE), is the world's first and only fullstream provider of integrated oilfield products, services and digital solutions. Drawing on a storied heritage of invention, BHGE harnesses the passion and experience of its people to enhance productivity across the oil and gas value chain.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Control Field Specialist - Industrial & Energy Technology (IET)

Job Requisition ID: R159662
Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Job Type: Full-time

Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Are you a Control system/Electrical Specialist looking for an interesting opportunity?
	Do you enjoy installation and maintenance?


Join our IET GTS Field Engineering team:


	Our IET team provides industry-leading products and services that optimize the extraction, production, and processing of energy. Our field services team support the on-site installation, maintenance, and planning of our industry-leading products for our global customers.


Partner with the best:


	As a Field Service Electrical Specialist, you will play a vital role onsite with our customers. Leading the installation, commissioning, and start-up activities, you will work with customers to troubleshoot issues and ensure safe and compliant installations.


Responsibilities
As a Field Service Control/Electrical Specialist, after an intense Training Program (ITP Program), you will be responsible for:


	Providing technical direction for activities to internal teams and skilled labor to ensure safety, compliance, and quality
	Overseeing, overhaul, Installations, troubleshooting and commissioning of our rotating equipment’s and products at customer sites
	Preparing and updating technical documentation, plans, daily and weekly briefing reports for customers and internal cross-functional teams
	Ensuring internal training, compliance and quality documentation is updated, followed, and completed according to company standards
	Properly manage company digital tools to retrieve technical documents and management of packing list, bill of material and drawings
	Properly deal with Electrical & Control schematics to provide clear instructions while at Customer sites.


Fuel Your Passion
To be successful in this role you will:


	Have completed a University or equivalent in Electric / Electronic Technical Degree or any other Engineering Degree.
	Possess a maximum of 3 years of experience in an Electrical/Electronic role in an energy or oil and gas environment (or similar) after Graduation.
	Must have completed NYSC. Exemption not accepted.
	Have basic knowledge of the main control systems as PLC or similar
	Have basic knowledge of Industrial Generators (multi-poles) and Electrical Motors.
	Have basic knowledge of Electric excitation systems and Variable Frequency Drivers (VFDs)
	Display a strong customer service-orientation mindset, experienced leading customers through tough decisions.
	Be skilled at communicating complex technical information to customers and teams with varying levels of technical understanding
	Ability to read technical drawings and schematics.
	Have a valid passport and have excellent verbal and written English
	Be ready to travel significantly (up to 70%) without geographical restriction, including offshore facilities


Work in a way that works for you:
We recognize that everyone is different and that the way in which people want to work and deliver at their best is different for everyone too. In this role, we can offer the following flexible working patterns:


	This role is working with our customer on-site. You will have the flexibility to work in line with clients working patterns.


Click here to apply online

 

2.) Integrated Solutions Well Engineer

Job Requisition ID: R159440
Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Job Type: Full-time
Industry: Engineering/Technology

Fuel your passion


	The Integrated Solutions Well Engineer performs all the well engineering activities within a project or Center of Excellence according to guidelines and processes set out in the Integrated Solutions Management System, international and/or local standards and regulations and customer requirements.
	The Integrated Solutions Well Engineer provides technical input to the well construction, completions, well intervention and abandonment phases, from the concept and tendering stage through project commencement, execution and project close out.
	During tendering phases, in addition to engineering work, the engineer will be supporting the commercial and procurement teams.
	During planning and execution stages, the engineer interfaces with stakeholders to ensure safety, well integrity and cost effective job execution.
	The Well Engineer ensures the well design and well program is prepared and detailed procedures are provided to the execution team; the Well Engineer is a key contributor during project execution providing follow up, technical input, and operational support.
	The Integrated Solutions Well Engineer will take the ownership of preparing basic well design, technical requirements, and operational plans to accomplish both Baker Hughes and customer’s technical and financial goals.
	The well engineer drives close engagement with service delivery, HSE, finance, commercial and sales, and other Baker Hughes product lines.


Responsibilities
As an Integrated Solutions Well Engineer, you will be responsible for:


	Ensuring all engineering activities are in line with HSE policies and procedures, and drive HSE culture by setting example through safe work practices. Conducting business according to Baker Hughes values
	Participating in tender bid preparation process for well engineering input.
	Participating in customer presentations & meetings (may include travel to other locations).
	Preparing of well design, well plan and operational procedures for construction, completion, intervention and/or abandonment of wells in area of responsibility.
	Defining technical services and third party requirements. Preparing operational time and cost estimates.
	Collaborating with internal and external parties, prepare risk register and contingency plans.
	Preparing and participating in peer reviews, peer assists and other quality assurance activities related to the project.
	Monitoring daily progress of job(s) or project(s) and monitor operations expenditure.
	Evaluating the application and implementation of best practices and technological advancements to Integrated Solutions operations.
	Communicating effectively with well site/project stakeholders as needed and make recommendations to drive flawless service delivery and operational excellence as per approved plans.
	Monitoring technical and operations KPIs and benchmark against offset well data and pre-defined well objectives.
	Participating in Root Cause Analysis investigation.
	Preparing all technical reports related to the project including End of Well Report, after action reviews, lessons learned summary.
	Complying with data management according to Integrated Solutions procedures.
	Keeping abreast of technological and engineering process advancements through affiliation with professional societies and participation in networks, workshops, seminars and training courses


Requirements
To be successful in this role you will:


	Have a Bachelor's degree preferably in Petroleum Engineering or Mechanical/Chemical Engineering with MS in Petroleum/Oil & Gas or Well Engineering.
	Have at least 5 years of relevant work experience in well construction, completions, intervention and/or optimization: the experience includes Plug & Abandonment Design, Well Integrity Management and Coiled Tubing Applications.
	Basic knowledge/exposure to specific applications: conventional rotary drilling, horizontal and/or multilateral wells, pad drilling, unconventional.
	Interpersonal skills to work well with colleagues across all businesses/cultures, achieve objectives through effective influencing, and establish credibility with senior leaders.
	Excellent analytics and problem-solving, including ability to form hypotheses and move forward in the absence of complete information.
	Excellent English oral and written communication skills, including ability to synthesize complex topics into clear and simple messages.
	Experience in the use of MS office suite, power BI or other analytical tools, well engineering and design software (for example: hydraulics, torque and drag, basic casing design).


Work in a way that works for you:


	We recognize that everyone is different and that the way in which people want to work and deliver at their best is different for everyone too.
	In this role, we can offer the following flexible working patterns:
	Working flexible hours - flexing the times when you work in the day to help you fit everything in and work when you are the most productive.


Working with us:


	Our people are at the heart of what we do at Baker Hughes. We know we are better when all of our people are developed, engaged and able to bring their whole authentic selves to work.
	We invest in the health and well-being of our workforce, train and reward talent and develop leaders at all levels to bring out the best in each other.


Working for you:
Our inventions have revolutionized energy for over a century. But to keep going forward tomorrow, we know we must push the boundaries today. We prioritize rewarding those who embrace change with a package that reflects how much we value their input. Join us, and you can expect:


	Contemporary work-life balance policies and wellbeing activities
	Comprehensive private medical care options
	Safety net of life insurance and disability programs
	Tailored financial programs
	Additional elected or voluntary benefits.


Click here to apply online

 

 

3.) Sales Specialist - Drilling & Completion Fluid

Job Requisition ID: R161926
Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Job Type: Full-time

Fuel Your Passion


	As a Sales Advisor, you will contribute to sales plan in a specific region. Defines sales strategy for targeted clients.


Responsibilities
As a Sales Advisor, you will be responsible for:


	Handling standardized products with pre-defined options requiring simple client advice.
	Contributing to Prices and conditions to have a certain degree of granularity. Works on the basis of predefined contracts price frameworks. Some latitude to deviate from prices and conditions.
	Handling diverse clients in a region.
	Cooperating with (technical) sales support to realize sales.
	Leading a department with medium-sized estimated orders.
	Handling local focus on Small-Medium size territory


Requirements
To be successful in this role you will:


	Have a Bachelor’s Degree in related discipline from an accredited university or relevant industry experience.
	Have a minimum of 5 years of experience in Sales.
	Have a technical background and understanding of Drilling & Completion Fluid
	Have an organized approach to problem solving with good analytical skills
	Be a natural, proactive communicator with good customer service skills and be fluent in English
	Demonstrate interpersonal and leadership skills
	Be skilled in functional use of MS Office Suite, with MS Project familiarity is required.
	Possess good communication skills.


Work in a way that works for you:


	We recognize that everyone is different and that the way in which people want to work and deliver at their best is different for everyone too. In this role, we can offer the following flexible working patterns:
	Working flexible hours - flexing the times when you work in the day to help you fit everything in and work when you are the most productive.


Working with us:


	Our people are at the heart of what we do at Baker Hughes. We know we are better when all of our people are developed, engaged and able to bring their whole authentic selves to work. We invest in the health and well-being of our workforce, train and reward talent and develop leaders at all levels to bring out the best in each other.


Working for you
Our inventions have revolutionized energy for over a century. But to keep going forward tomorrow, we know we must push the boundaries today. We prioritize rewarding those who embrace change with a package that reflects how much we value their input. Join us, and you can expect:


	Contemporary work-life balance policies and wellbeing activities
	Comprehensive private medical care options
	Safety net of life insurance and disability programs
	Tailored financial programs
	Additional elected or voluntary benefits


Click here to apply online    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "Cybercollect Limited is a newly established debt collection agency based in Lagos. We provide professional debt recovery services for banks, microfinance institutions, and telecommunications companies in Nigeria. Our mission is to ensure effective debt collection in compliance with legal and ethical standards, helping creditors minimize losses while supporting debtors in finding optimal solutions to their financial challenges.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Inbound Call Center Operator

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are looking for a professional and customer-oriented inbound call center operator with at least 2 years of experience to join our growing microfinance team in Nigeria.
	The ideal candidate has excellent communication skills, the ability to handle a high volume of calls, and preferably hands-on experience with inbound calls in debt collection or loan servicing.
	This role is critical to ensuring excellent customer service, efficient handling of inquiries, and compliance with company policies and regulatory standards.&nbsp;
	(Debt Collection Experience Preferred) &mdash; Nigeria | Fintech / Microfinance


Key Responsibilities


	Handle incoming calls from customers regarding loans, repayments, account status, and general inquiries
	Provide accurate information about products, payment options, and policies
	Support customers with payment-related issues and assist in resolving problems
	Communicate with customers professionally and empathetically, especially in sensitive financial situations
	Document all calls and accurately update information in CRM systems
	Meet key performance indicators (KPIs), such as call quality, response time, and resolution rates


Requirements


	Minimum of 2 years of experience as a call center operator (inbound calls required)
	Experience in debt collection, loan servicing, or financial services support is preferred
	Excellent spoken English skills (knowledge of additional local languages is an advantage)
	Ability to remain calm and professional in stressful situations
	Strong listening, negotiation, and problem-solving skills
	Basic computer skills and experience with CRM systems or call center software


Preferred Qualifications:


	Experience in microfinance, lending, fintech, or banking is highly desirable
	Knowledge of debt collection regulations and customer service standards
	Experience working in high-volume call environments with structured KPIs


What You Will Do:


	Handle inbound calls related to loans, repayments, and account support
	Provide accurate information and efficiently resolve customer inquiries
	Assist customers in identifying payment solutions and next steps
	Maintain detailed call documentation in CRM systems
	Achieve KPIs and call quality standards


What We Are Looking For:


	Minimum 2 years of inbound call center experience (required)
	Experience in debt collection or loan servicing (a strong advantage)
	Excellent English proficiency; local languages are a plus
	Strong communication, listening, and problem-solving skills
	Ability to remain calm and professional under pressure.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary and performance-based bonuses
	Career growth opportunities in a fast-growing fintech company
	Professional training and onboarding support
	A collaborative, results-driven team culture
	Structured onboarding and training
	Supportive, performance-oriented team environment.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resume along with a short cover letter highlighting your experience in inbound calls and debt collection to:&nbsp;recruitment_cybebird@cyberbird.tech&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note
Candidate Instructions:
Please submit your resume along with a brief description of your relevant experience, with a focus on inbound call handling and accounts receivable / debt collection (if applicable).",

Inbound Call Center Operator Job at Cybercollect Limited




        
    

        
        Cybercollect Limited is a newly established debt collection agency based in Lagos. We provide professional debt recovery services for banks, microfinance institutions, and telecommunications companies in Nigeria. Our mission is to ensure effective debt collection in compliance with legal and ethical standards, helping creditors minimize losses while supporting debtors in finding optimal solutions to their financial challenges.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Inbound Call Center Operator

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are looking for a professional and customer-oriented inbound call center operator with at least 2 years of experience to join our growing microfinance team in Nigeria.
	The ideal candidate has excellent communication skills, the ability to handle a high volume of calls, and preferably hands-on experience with inbound calls in debt collection or loan servicing.
	This role is critical to ensuring excellent customer service, efficient handling of inquiries, and compliance with company policies and regulatory standards. 
	(Debt Collection Experience Preferred) — Nigeria | Fintech / Microfinance


Key Responsibilities


	Handle incoming calls from customers regarding loans, repayments, account status, and general inquiries
	Provide accurate information about products, payment options, and policies
	Support customers with payment-related issues and assist in resolving problems
	Communicate with customers professionally and empathetically, especially in sensitive financial situations
	Document all calls and accurately update information in CRM systems
	Meet key performance indicators (KPIs), such as call quality, response time, and resolution rates


Requirements


	Minimum of 2 years of experience as a call center operator (inbound calls required)
	Experience in debt collection, loan servicing, or financial services support is preferred
	Excellent spoken English skills (knowledge of additional local languages is an advantage)
	Ability to remain calm and professional in stressful situations
	Strong listening, negotiation, and problem-solving skills
	Basic computer skills and experience with CRM systems or call center software


Preferred Qualifications:


	Experience in microfinance, lending, fintech, or banking is highly desirable
	Knowledge of debt collection regulations and customer service standards
	Experience working in high-volume call environments with structured KPIs


What You Will Do:


	Handle inbound calls related to loans, repayments, and account support
	Provide accurate information and efficiently resolve customer inquiries
	Assist customers in identifying payment solutions and next steps
	Maintain detailed call documentation in CRM systems
	Achieve KPIs and call quality standards


What We Are Looking For:


	Minimum 2 years of inbound call center experience (required)
	Experience in debt collection or loan servicing (a strong advantage)
	Excellent English proficiency; local languages are a plus
	Strong communication, listening, and problem-solving skills
	Ability to remain calm and professional under pressure.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary and performance-based bonuses
	Career growth opportunities in a fast-growing fintech company
	Professional training and onboarding support
	A collaborative, results-driven team culture
	Structured onboarding and training
	Supportive, performance-oriented team environment.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resume along with a short cover letter highlighting your experience in inbound calls and debt collection to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note
Candidate Instructions:
Please submit your resume along with a brief description of your relevant experience, with a focus on inbound call handling and accounts receivable / debt collection (if applicable).    

    

        Click here to Apply
